questions.q: Who founded the Mughal Empire in 1526 CE?
questions.explanation: The Mughal Empire was founded by Babur after his victory at Panipat in 1526.

questions.q: In which battle did Babur win control to found the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: Babur founded the empire after winning the First Battle of Panipat in 1526.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor is famed for building the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal at Agra in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor is often called 'the Great' for his reforms and tolerance?
questions.explanation: Akbar, the third emperor, is celebrated as Akbar the Great.

questions.q: Until which year did the Mughal Empire endure in some form?
questions.explanation: The Mughal Empire endured in some form until 1857, when the last emperor was deposed.

questions.q: The Mughal dynasty descended from which Central Asian conqueror through Babur?
questions.explanation: Babur was a Timurid, descended from Timur, and also from Genghis Khan.

questions.q: Which city served as a principal Mughal capital under both Akbar and Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Agra, on the Yamuna, was a principal Mughal capital, home to the Agra Fort and Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor extended the empire to its greatest territorial extent?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb expanded the empire to its largest extent before its long decline.

questions.q: The Mughal Empire is especially renowned for which cultural achievement?
questions.explanation: The Mughals are renowned for monumental architecture, including the Taj Mahal and great forts.

questions.q: Which emperor was the second ruler of the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: Humayun, son of Babur, was the second Mughal emperor.

questions.q: Which interruption separated the reigns of the first two Mughal emperors and the empire's later consolidation?
options: The Sur dynasty interregnum under Sher Shah Suri
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri ousted Humayun and ruled as the Sur dynasty before Humayun reclaimed the throne in 1555.

questions.q: Akbar's administrative system that ranked nobles by numerical grades was called what?
questions.explanation: Akbar organised the nobility through the mansabdari system of numerical ranks.

questions.q: Akbar's land-revenue reform, refined by Raja Todar Mal, was known as what?
questions.explanation: Akbar's revenue system, the zabt or dahsala settlement, was developed under Raja Todar Mal.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor reigned from 1605 to 1627 and received the English envoy Sir Thomas Roe?
questions.explanation: Jahangir, who reigned 1605 to 1627, received Sir Thomas Roe's embassy from England.

questions.q: The administrative gazetteer of Akbar's empire, the Ain-i-Akbari, was written by whom?
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl, Akbar's minister, compiled the Ain-i-Akbari as part of the Akbarnama.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor moved the capital to a new walled city, Shahjahanabad, in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built Shahjahanabad in Delhi, the area known today as Old Delhi.

questions.q: Akbar's experimental syncretic faith was called what?
questions.explanation: Akbar promoted Din-i-Ilahi, a syncretic faith, alongside his policy of sulh-i-kul (universal peace).

questions.q: Which official Mughal court language was used for administration and literature?
questions.explanation: Persian was the official language of the Mughal court and administration.

questions.q: Which jewelled throne, commissioned by Shah Jahan, symbolised Mughal splendour before being carried off in 1739?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's Peacock Throne was looted by Nadir Shah in 1739.

questions.q: Which Persian invader sacked Delhi in 1739, hastening Mughal decline?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah of Persia sacked Delhi in 1739 and seized the Peacock Throne and Koh-i-Noor.

questions.q: The order of the first six 'Grand Mughal' emperors was Babur, Humayun, Akbar, Jahangir, Shah Jahan, and who?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was the sixth of the great Mughal emperors.

questions.q: Who was the last Mughal emperor, deposed by the British in 1857?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah II, known as Zafar, was the last Mughal emperor, exiled after the 1857 revolt.

questions.q: Aurangzeb died in 1707; in which long-running theatre of war had he spent his final decades campaigning?
options: The Punjab against the Sikhs alone
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb spent his last decades in costly Deccan campaigns, largely against the Marathas.

questions.q: The 'Battle of Samugarh' in 1658 settled which Mughal contest?
options: The war of succession won by Aurangzeb over Dara Shikoh
questions.explanation: At Samugarh (1658), Aurangzeb defeated his brother Dara Shikoh in the war of succession.

questions.q: Which Mughal prince and scholar, defeated by Aurangzeb, was known for translating the Upanishads into Persian?
questions.explanation: Dara Shikoh, Aurangzeb's eldest brother, sponsored Persian translations of the Upanishads.

questions.q: The decline-era period after 1707 in which the empire shrank rapidly is sometimes dated to the death of which emperor?
questions.explanation: The empire's swift decline is conventionally dated from Aurangzeb's death in 1707.

questions.q: Akbar reportedly abolished which tax on non-Muslims, a measure later reimposed by Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: Akbar abolished the jizya tax on non-Muslims; Aurangzeb reimposed it in 1679.

questions.q: The Mughal painting atelier reached a famed peak of naturalism under which emperor, a noted connoisseur of art?
questions.explanation: Mughal miniature painting flourished under the connoisseur emperor Jahangir.

questions.q: Which 1739 battle near Karnal saw the Mughal army crushed by Nadir Shah before the sack of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Nadir Shah defeated the Mughals at the Battle of Karnal in 1739 before sacking Delhi.

questions.q: By the late 18th century, the Mughal emperor at Delhi had effectively become a pensioner under the protection of which rising power before the British?
questions.explanation: By the late 1700s the Mughal emperor was a protected figurehead, dominated by Maratha chief Mahadji Scindia before British ascendancy.
